title: Adding a new container to an old pod results in inconsitencies
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I have a pod that was created about a year ago. Today I wanted to update one of the containers, so I removed the outdated container, create a new one with the `--pod` option set to the pod's name. This kind of works. The new container is added, but it is not consistent with the other container in the pod. I've noticed two things:

1) the hostname is set to the container's id, not the pod's name.
2) The port mapping is not set up on the new container.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Have an old pod.
2. Create a new container within that pod.

Create command according to `podman inspect`
```
 "CreateCommand": [
            "podman",
            "pod",
            "create",
            "-p",
            "8080:80",
            "--name",
            "mypod"
        ],
```

Command I'm using to create a new container in that pod

`podman create -v ./:/var/www/html:z --name website-php --pod mypod myimage`

**Describe the results you received:**
The new container is created but the hostname is not correctly set to the pod's name, and the port mappings are non-existent.

**Describe the results you expected:**
hostname should be set to the pod's name, and ports should be mapped just like in the pod. (Currently I have 8080 mapped to 80)

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
If I create a new pod, and create my new container in that pod, everything works as expected. I've been able to reproduce this same issue with two different pods, both are almost equally old (a bit over 1 year)
**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      3.4.7
API Version:  3.4.7
Go Version:   go1.16.15
Built:        Thu Apr 21 13:38:09 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```
Description  : podman (Pod Manager) is a fully featured container engine that is a simple
             : daemonless tool.  podman provides a Docker-CLI comparable command line that
             : eases the transition from other container engines and allows the management of
             : pods, containers and images.  Simply put: alias docker=podman.
             : Most podman commands can be run as a regular user, without requiring
             : additional privileges.
             : 
             : podman uses Buildah(1) internally to create container images.
             : Both tools share image (not container) storage, hence each can use or
             : manipulate images (but not containers) created by the other.
             : 
             : Manage Pods, Containers and Container Images
             : podman Simple management tool for pods, containers and images
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


No, my distro doesn't offer a newer version of podman.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body: Please test this with podman 4.3 or 4.2 at least. We cannot really support these old versions upstream.

I'm afraid I won't be able to do that. My server is not providing a newer version of podman, and while I could install a newer version locally, I would not be able to create a new pod that is over a year old. And this problem is not affecting new pods in the server, only the older ones.
I found I can probably install a newer version https://podman.io/blogs/2022/03/06/why_no_podman4_f35.html I'll try this and report back.
I was finally able to install podman v 4.2.1 and the problem persists.

Do you know with what version the podman was originally created? The age shouldn't matter just the version itself.

It is likely that the only solution is to recreate the pods. It is hard to find the cause and IMO not really worth to search for given that new pods work just fine.

I believe they were originally made with podman 3.4.7, since that was the version that was running before on the server. I can see how this is a rather hard-to-track issue, and how it may not be worth the effort. I wish I had the time and knowledge to debug this myself if anything just to satisfy my own curiosity.
